evals.prompt: re-org the recall topics, make them coherent and extract general topic out as a separated topic
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, runs a read-only analysis of the current branch overlay first (scripted inventory + delegated judgment), presents a per-category plan (identity/index/size/extraction/promote-candidates), and does not write anything before category-by-category confirmation. Recognizes deliberately-kept falsified-lead records as do-not-delete.
expectations: Defaults to branch scope (current branch overlay), not the whole project or all projects
Runs a read-only analysis before proposing any change (no writes in a dry run)
Uses or references the scripted inventory (reorg-inventory.sh) rather than reading all 56 files blindly
Presents the plan grouped into categories (identity / index / size / merge-extract / promote-candidates)
Proposes extracting a shared/general principle into its own topic with cross-links
Identifies deliberately-kept falsified-lead records and does NOT propose deleting them
States that a snapshot backup would be taken before execution
Flags branch topics that look project-general as promote-candidates deferred to /promote

evals.prompt: the recall knowledge index is a mess now, some topics are huge and I think a few say the same thing. clean it up
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers on an implicit cleanup request. Identifies oversized topics and routes them compact-before-split; identifies overlapping/duplicate topics as merge candidates; rewrites the index coherently. Snapshots a backup before any write and gates writes per category.
expectations: Triggers on the implicit cleanup phrasing without the literal word 'reorg'
Identifies the oversized topic files (over the 200-line limit)
Routes oversized files compact-before-split (does not jump straight to splitting)
Identifies overlapping/duplicate topics as merge candidates
Checks the index against the files both ways (dead links and missing entries)
States that a snapshot backup would be taken before execution
Presents the plan per category rather than applying everything at once

evals.prompt: one of my recall topic files grew past the size limit — can you get it back under without losing anything?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers and applies the size policy: delete disproven/stale content first (but NOT deliberately-kept falsified-lead records), then lossless compaction, and only split as a last resort. Reports lines saved and confirms the file is under the limit.
expectations: Triggers on a size-recovery request
Applies the compact-before-split order: delete-stale, then lossless compact, then split only as last resort
Distinguishes deliberately-kept falsified-lead records from deletable stale content
Preserves all load-bearing facts (the 'without losing anything' constraint)
Identifies the specific oversized file(s) and estimates lines saved / confirms under limit
